question_text: As closely as you can recall, please choose the approximate age at which your child did the following things (please use a baby book if you kept one): smiled
notes: Different versions of this question over time had different ways of asking about age at which milestone was met. Responses were recoded to integers, where the integer equals the age reported converted into months. Note that for some, the recoding might reflect exact age (eg, participant said "2 months" and value is "2"), while for others it may not reflect exact age (eg, if participant reported "10 years old" this maps to "120", even if participant was not exactly 120 months).

question_text:
Based on response to the question: How does [participant name] currently get around? Please select all that apply. [Walks independently without any supports |Walks using Knee/Ankle Foot Orthosis (K/AFO), Supramalleolar Orthosis (SMO), or other orthotic devices | Self-propelled manual wheelchair | Self-operated motorized wheelchair | Wheelchair or transit/mobility stroller pushed by someone | Cane | Crutches | Uses a walker | Mobility scooter | Gait trainer | Crawls | Scoots or bottom shuffles (uses legs and sometimes arms to move while sitting) | Other | Not yet mobile, but sits without support | None of the above
 See also best_latest_walking
notes: missing (null) walking or talking values could mean either that they had already reported the attainment of that skill prior to the follow-up survey and did not need to report on it again, or that the question was skipped. It is recommended to consult the Core Descriptive Variables file for best_latest_walking/talking values.

source_name: Simons Foundation dataset data dictionary (DS0000032 v14)
question_text:
Based on response to the question: How does {participantContext.firstName} currently communicate? Please select all that apply. [Gestures (for example, pointing and reaching) | Written communication | Alternative and augmentative communication or AAC (for example, tablets with communication applications, picture exchange systems) | Babbles or uses sounds | Uses single words (on their own, not immediately repeating other people) | Combines words into phrases or short sentences | Uses longer sentences on their own (not repeating other people) and is able to talk about events or objects that are not in their current surroundings | Sign language | Other | Does not communicate
 See also best_latest_talking
notes: missing (null) walking or talking values could mean either that they had already reported the attainment of that skill prior to the follow-up survey and did not need to report on it again, or that the question was skipped. It is recommended to consult the Core Descriptive Variables file for best_latest_walking/talking values.

 single_intragen = a copy number variant resulting in deletion of the gene, involving only the Searchlight gene in question
